config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa mayoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan perlakuan adil terhadap tiap orang. Kalau prioritasnya terlalu berat ke masyarakat, saya khawatir itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should generally take priority over individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case: public safety, disease control, and basic laws do require some limits, but those limits need to be narrow, justified, and accountable. What matters most is that society serves people, not that people are sacrificed for an abstract idea of society. If we lose strong individual rights, the majority can too easily silence minorities and call it progress.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau membenarkan aturan yang tidak adil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dilanggar semaunya, karena tanpa itu masyarakat justru mudah berubah jadi alat mayoritas atau penguasa. Yang masuk akal menurut saya adalah mencari keseimbangan: masyarakat boleh membatasi hak individu hanya bila benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights in any broad or default sense. From an American perspective, history makes me wary of that claim because “the common good” has repeatedly been used to justify segregation, censorship, forced conformity, and other abuses against minorities. I do accept that rights can be limited in narrow cases like public health or immediate safety, but those limits should be exceptional, tightly justified, and subject to real oversight. If society is allowed to outrank the individual too easily, then the majority gets a blank check to call oppression “responsibility.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, dalih “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau aturan yang tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau kesehatan umum, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat terbatas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji secara transparan. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat malah mudah berubah menjadi alat mayoritas atau penguasa untuk menekan orang yang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American point of view, that idea has too often been a cover for discrimination, censorship, and government overreach, especially when the majority decides its convenience matters more than minority rights. I do think there are narrow cases where rights can be limited for public safety or health, but those limits have to be exceptional, proportional, and accountable. If society is treated as automatically more important than the individual, then the people with the least power are usually the ones who pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
